sections.label: Section 1.147
sections.original: ἴδʼ , ἀτρεμαῖον ὡς ὑπόροφον φέρω
sections.translation: See , how soft and low I drop my voice .

sections.label: Section 1.148b
sections.original: ναί , οὕτως ·
sections.translation: Yes , do so ; approach now , softly , softly !

sections.label: Section 1.150
sections.original: λόγον ἀπόδος ἐφʼ ὅ τι χρέος ἐμόλετέ ποτε .
sections.translation: Give me an account of why you have come here . For at last he has lain down , and sleeps .

sections.label: Section 1.153
sections.original: πῶς ἔχει ; λόγου μετάδος , ὦ φίλα ·
sections.translation: How is he ? You give us an account , my dear ; what has happened , what misfortune ?

sections.label: Section 1.155
sections.original: ἔτι μὲν ἐμπνέει , βραχὺ δʼ ἀναστένει .
sections.translation: He is still breathing , but his moans grow feeble .

sections.label: Section 1.156
sections.original: τί φῄς ; ὦ τάλας .
sections.translation: What are you saying ? turning to Orestes . Unhappy Orestes !

sections.label: Section 1.158
sections.original: ὀλεῖς , εἰ βλέφαρα κινήσεις
sections.translation: You will kill him , if you disturb him from the sweet sleep he now enjoys .

sections.label: Section 1.160
sections.original: μέλεος ἐχθίστων θεόθεν ἐργμάτων ,
sections.translation: Poor sufferer , for his hateful deeds , inspired by a god !

sections.label: Section 1.162
sections.original: φεῦ μόχθων .
sections.translation: Ah , misery ! Injustice it was , after all , from an unjust mouth , when Loxias on the tripod of Themis

sections.label: Section 1.165
sections.original: φόνον ὁ Λοξίας ἐμᾶς ματέρος .
sections.translation: decreed my mother ’ s most unnatural murder .

sections.label: Section 2.166
sections.original: ὁρᾷς ; ἐν πέπλοισι κινεῖ δέμας .
sections.translation: Do you see ? He stirs beneath his robe !

sections.label: Section 2.167
sections.original: σὺ γάρ νιν , ὦ τάλαινα ,
sections.translation: Alas ! Your noisy chatter has roused him from his sleep .

sections.label: Section 2.169
sections.original: εὕδειν μὲν οὖν ἔδοξα .
sections.translation: No , I think he is asleep .

sections.label: Section 2.170
sections.original: οὐκ ἀφʼ ἡμῶν , οὐκ ἀπʼ οἴκων
sections.translation: Leave us , go away from the house ! circle back again ! cease this noise !

sections.label: Section 2.173
sections.original: ὑπνώσσει .
sections.translation: He is asleep .

sections.label: Section 2.173b
sections.original: λέγεις εὖ .
sections.translation: You are right . [*] O Lady Night ,

sections.label: Section 2.175
sections.original: ὑπνοδότειρα τῶν πολυπόνων βροτῶν ,
sections.translation: giver of sleep to hard-working mortals , come from Erebus , come , wing your way to the palace of Agamemnon .

sections.label: Section 2.180
sections.original: ὑπὸ γὰρ ἀλγέων ὑπό τε συμφορᾶς
sections.translation: For with misery and woe we are lost , we are gone .

sections.label: Section 2.181
sections.original: διοιχόμεθʼ , οἰχόμεθα . κτύπον ἠγάγετʼ · οὐχὶ σῖγα
sections.translation: There ! To the Chorus . that noise again ! Do be still and keep the sound of your voice

sections.label: Section 2.185
sections.original: ἄνα κέλαδον ἀπὸ λέχεος ἥ-
sections.translation: away from his couch ; let him enjoy his sleep in peace , my dear !

sections.label: Section 2.187
sections.original: θρόει τίς κακῶν τελευτὰ μένει .
sections.translation: Tell me , what end of troubles awaits him .

sections.label: Section 2.188
sections.original: θανεῖν θανεῖν , τί δʼ ἄλλο ;
sections.translation: Death , death ; what else ? For he has no desire for food .

sections.label: Section 2.190
sections.original: πρόδηλος ἆρʼ ὁ πότμος .
sections.translation: Then his destiny is already clear .

sections.label: Section 2.191
sections.original: ἐξέθυσʼ ὁ Φοῖβος ἡμᾶς
sections.translation: Phoebus offered us up for sacrifice , when he ordered the pitiable , unnatural murder of our mother , who killed our father .

sections.label: Section 2.194
sections.original: δίκᾳ μέν .
sections.translation: It was just .

sections.label: Section 2.194b
sections.original: καλῶς δʼ οὔ .
sections.translation: But it was not well done .

sections.label: Section 2.195
sections.original: ἔκανες ἔθανες , ὦ
sections.translation: You killed and were killed , my mother ! and you have slain a father and your own children ;

sections.label: Section 2.200
sections.original: ὀλόμεθʼ ἰσονέκυες , ὀλόμεθα .
sections.translation: for we are dead or as good as dead . You are in your grave , and the greater part of my life is spent in weeping and wailing ,

sections.label: Section 2.205
sections.original: δάκρυσί τʼ ἐννυχίοις , ἄγαμος
sections.translation: and tears at night ; unmarried , childless , I drag out forever a joyless existence .

sections.label: Section 208
sections.original: ὅρα παροῦσα , παρθένʼ Ἠλέκτρα , πέλας ,
sections.translation: Electra , you are nearby ; see whether your brother has not died without your knowing it ;

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: οὐ γάρ μʼ ἀρέσκει τῷ λίαν παρειμένῳ .
sections.translation: for I do not like his utter prostration .

sections.label: Section 211
sections.original: ὦ φίλον ὕπνου θέλγητρον , ἐπίκουρον νόσου ,
sections.translation: Sweet charm of sleep , savior in sickness , how sweetly you came to me , how needed ! Revered forgetfulness of troubles , how wise a goddess you are , and invoked by every suffering soul !

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: πόθεν ποτʼ ἦλθον δεῦρο ; πῶς δʼ ἀφικόμην ;
sections.translation: Addressing Electra . Where have I come from ? How am I here ? For I have lost all previous recollection and remember nothing .

sections.label: Section 217
sections.original: ὦ φίλταθʼ , ὥς μʼ ηὔφρανας εἰς ὕπνον πεσών .
sections.translation: My dearest , how glad I was to see you fall asleep ! Do you want me take you in my arms and lift your body ?

sections.label: Section 219
sections.original: λαβοῦ λαβοῦ δῆτʼ , ἐκ δʼ ὄμορξον ἀθλίου
sections.translation: Take , oh ! take me in your arms , and from this sufferer ’ s mouth

sections.label: Section 220
sections.original: στόματος ἀφρώδη πέλανον ὀμμάτων τʼ ἐμῶν .
sections.translation: and eyes wipe off the flakes of foam .

sections.label: Section 221
sections.original: ἰδού · τὸ δούλευμʼ ἡδύ , κοὐκ ἀναίνομαι
sections.translation: There ! The service is sweet , and I do not refuse to tend a brother ’ s limbs with a sister ’ s hand .

sections.label: Section 223
sections.original: ὑπόβαλε πλευροῖς πλευρά , καὐχμώδη κόμην
sections.translation: Prop me up , your side to mine ; brush the matted hair from my face , for I see dimly .

sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: ὦ βοστρύχων πινῶδες ἄθλιον κάρα ,
sections.translation: Ah , poor head , how dirty your hair ! How savage you look , remaining so long unwashed !

sections.label: Section 227
sections.original: κλῖνόν μʼ ἐς εὐνὴν αὖθις · ὅταν ἀνῇ νόσος
sections.translation: Put me once more upon the couch ; whenever the madness leaves me , I am unnerved and weak .

sections.label: Section 229
sections.original: ἰδού . φίλον τοι τῷ νοσοῦντι δέμνιον ,
sections.translation: As she lays him down . There ! His couch is welcome to the sick man ,

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: ἀνιαρὸν ὂν τὸ κτῆμʼ , ἀναγκαῖον δʼ ὅμως .
sections.translation: a painful possession , but a necessary one .

sections.label: Section 231
sections.original: αὖθίς μʼ ἐς ὀρθὸν στῆσον , ἀνακύκλει δέμας ·
sections.translation: Set me upright once again , turn my body round ; it is their helplessness that makes the sick so hard to please .

sections.label: Section 233
sections.original: ἦ κἀπὶ γαίας ἁρμόσαι πόδας θέλεις ,
sections.translation: Will you set your feet upon the ground and take a step at last ? Change is always pleasant .

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: μάλιστα · δόξαν γὰρ τόδʼ ὑγιείας ἔχει .
sections.translation: Oh , yes ; for that has a semblance of health ; and the semblance is preferable , though it is far from the truth .

sections.label: Section 237
sections.original: ἄκουε δὴ νῦν , ὦ κασίγνητον κάρα ,
sections.translation: Hear me now , my brother , while the Furies permit you to use your senses .

sections.label: Section 239
sections.original: λέξεις τι καινόν · κεἰ μὲν εὖ , χάριν φέρεις ·
sections.translation: You have news to tell ; if it is good , you do me a kindness ;

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: εἰ δʼ ἐς βλάβην τινʼ , ἅλις ἔχω τὸ δυστυχεῖν .
sections.translation: but if it tends to my hurt , I have suffered enough .

sections.label: Section 241
sections.original: Μενέλαος ἥκει , σοῦ κασίγνητος πατρός ,
sections.translation: Menelaus , your father ’ s brother , has come ; his ships are moored in Nauplia .

sections.label: Section 243
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ; ἥκει φῶς ἐμοῖς καὶ σοῖς κακοῖς
sections.translation: What did you say ? Has he come to be a light in our troubles , a man of our own family , who owes gratitude to our father ?

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: ἥκει — τὸ πιστὸν τόδε λόγων ἐμῶν δέχου —
sections.translation: He has come , and is bringing Helen from the walls of Troy — accept this as proof of what I say .

sections.label: Section 247
sections.original: εἰ μόνος ἐσώθη , μᾶλλον ἂν ζηλωτὸς ἦν ·
sections.translation: If he had returned alone in safety , he would be more enviable ; but if he is bringing his wife , he has come with great mischief .

sections.label: Section 249
sections.original: ἐπίσημον ἔτεκε Τυνδάρεως ἐς τὸν ψόγον
sections.translation: Tyndareus begot a race of daughters notorious for blame ,

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: γένος θυγατέρων δυσκλεές τʼ ἀνʼ Ἑλλάδα .
sections.translation: infamous throughout Hellas .

sections.label: Section 251
sections.original: σύ νυν διάφερε τῶν κακῶν · ἔξεστι γάρ ·
sections.translation: Then you be different from that evil brood , for you can be ; and not only in words , but also in heart .

sections.label: Section 253
sections.original: οἴμοι , κασίγνητʼ , ὄμμα σὸν ταράσσεται ,
sections.translation: Ah ! brother , your eye is growing wild , and in a moment you are turning mad again , when you were just now sane .

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: ὦ μῆτερ , ἱκετεύω σε , μὴ ʼπίσειέ μοι
sections.translation: Mother , I implore you ! Do not shake at me those maidens with their bloodshot eyes and snaky hair . Here they are , close by , to leap on me !

sections.label: Section 258
sections.original: μένʼ , ὦ ταλαίπωρʼ , ἀτρέμα σοῖς ἐν δεμνίοις ·
sections.translation: Lie still , poor sufferer , on your couch ; your eye sees nothing , you only imagine that you recognize them .

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: ὦ Φοῖβʼ , ἀποκτενοῦσί μʼ αἱ κυνώπιδες
sections.translation: O Phoebus ! they will kill me , the hounds of hell , death ’ s priestesses with glaring eyes , the dreadful goddesses .

sections.label: Section 262
sections.original: οὔτοι μεθήσω · χεῖρα δʼ ἐμπλέξασʼ ἐμὴν
sections.translation: I will not let you go ; but with arms twined round you , I will prevent your piteous leaping .
